reviews.review_text:
I am a lifelong Harry Potter fan. I’ve been waiting for this game since the 2018 leaks, but it's an immense disappointment. To put it simply: it’s feels like it's a high-budget fan-fiction game that prioritizes aesthetics over substance.

The castle looks whimsical, but the layout is a mess of questionable design choices that ignore established lore, which in turn makes it less magical and believable (e.g., the Great Staircase that pops in and out based on your movement, or the Chamber of Secrets entrance in the dungeons instead of the second floor).

The story is bland, unimaginative, and even creatively bankrupt. It doesn't just draw inspiration from the books; it lazily copies every major plot trope. You are a 'Chosen One' with a unique ability (Ancient Magic) who saves the wizarding world from a terrible threat, complete with a final battle at the castle and the death of a mentor. It even drops the Deathly Hallows into the story with zero narrative weight. It's more like a failed museum of Harry Potter memorabilia than a story that has something to say.

The game is a textbook example of ludonarrative dissonance: the story says one thing, but the gameplay does another. You are told to combat poachers, yet you spend the game poaching animals for their resources and selling them for profit. You are a student fighting evil and hatred, yet you can cast Unforgivable Curses and kill people without a single reaction from your peers or professors. You can't even hit your fellow students with your spells, something that was possible in the previous games.

Avalanche claimed to be fans of this universe who prioritized attention to detail, yet throughout the game, your choices have zero impact. The only exception is your house selection, which grants an exclusive but tiny quest that ultimately leads to the same outcome. Beyond that, they didn't even bother to update the House Point hourglasses to create tension or show that time is passing. You simply win the House Cup by default at the end regardless of your actions (yet another trope taken from J.K.R.).

The gameplay is fundamentally weak. When you aren't wading through boring dialogue, you're trapped in a loop of tedious, repetitive collectibles. It feels like the Order of the Phoenix game and it's "Room of Rewards" but significantly worse. The map is massive, yet it’s an empty shell filled with nothing but useless items meant to be crossed off a checklist. The combat is equally uninspired, consisting mostly of mindless basic-attack spamming, occasionally interrupted by a dodge or a special ability/spell. It’s a repetitive slog, especially when you’re forced to fight the same handful of enemy types with almost no variety throughout the entire game. The first hours were nice, but the experience quickly become stale. 

Yes, the game captures the look of the Wizarding World perfectly, but it lacks the soul. It ignores the details that made this universe special in favor of a formulaic, open-world grind. It’s a beautiful shell, but for a lore-heavy franchise, it’s a failure of imagination. It feels like the developers didn't know what to do with their game, struggling to find its identity or anything pertinent to bring to the franchise. That's why I'm very disappointed. Maybe they should've made a video game adaptation of the books instead.

I loved this game. I spent so much time exploring the world. The world is beautiful.

But I wish there was more whimsy in the game. Everything in the game took itself way too seriously that I think they forgot the source material came from a children’s book. For example, there’s Zonko’s Joke Shop, but we never get the chance to do anything inside it for any quest (except for that one Garreth quest, and even then, we were in the basement instead of the actual shop). I would’ve loved being able to buy prank devices from Zonko’s, like Dungbombs and Fanged Frisbees, and prank other NPCs — and actually have the NPCs react to the player’s shenanigans.

Curfews should’ve also been added to the game. These are students, after all. I expected there to be penalties for sneaking around at night. There’s even an entire class dedicated to detention, yet we never actually get in trouble. There’s a hospital wing, but we never really get injured in a way that makes it meaningful. So are these places just there for decoration? It feels like such a waste not to utilise the spaces the dev team already designed.

Most of the characters felt flat (except maybe Sebastian and Poppy). There are all these characters like Leander, Lenora, Arthur, Everett, etc., but they weren’t fully utilised in the story, so as players, we don’t really get to care about them at all. I wish each introduced character had “Friendship Level events” or “Heart events” similar to Stardew Valley. When you reach a certain level of friendship with a character, you unlock their backstory or even gain benefits from building a closer relationship with them. Romance can be optional. Just give us something that makes us care about these side characters.

When we first enter the game, the castle feels magical and full of things to explore. Every nook and cranny of the castle is detailed, and I spent hours just looking at the decorations, paintings, and the mystique of it all. Until I started exploring outside the castle. That’s when it became a bummer. The NPCs outside felt flat and uninteresting. Don’t even get me started on the other half of the map. The world was wide and vast, but it felt so empty. It was disappointing.

I also wish there were more achievements to unlock so I’d have more reasons to keep playing. Even silly achievements similar to “Cast Flipendo on 20 cows” — things like “Pet 50 cats” or other fun little interactions. Those kinds of small details make the world feel more alive and rewarding to explore.

I did enjoy my time playing this game, but I definitely expected more. I wish I could’ve spent more time simply enjoying the wizarding world itself.

Still, I can’t wait for HL 2. Hopefully, the next game gives us even more to see and do! Please make us actually attend classes in the next game! Similar to the game Bully (2006) where for each class we successfully attend, we get an ability or perks.

Gameplay & mechanics: Very nice.
Graphics: Great.
Music: Good.
Script & characters: Very poor, unfortunately.

The story felt poorly written. I barely felt any emotional connection to the characters.

Also, I didn’t like how the game kept putting transgender witches and elves in my face throughout the game. I’m not against nontraditional genders, but I don’t like when people aggressively push or market these things.

So, basically, my wife enjoys it. I enjoyed it. It has a nice little cozy vibe of fairy tale game, there is some depth and complexity to its combat system, etc.
Dialogues are awful, story is questionable, some of the characters are grotesquely unbelievable, but the atmosphere is so good.

as an avid hp and fb fan, this game left me utterly amazed and gobsmacked. the 1-to-1 representation of the hogwarts castle is an absolute chef's kiss. the graphics on this game are insane. i could not achieve the absolute maximum graphics natively on my laptop, but i managed to run it brilliantly by setting my resolution to full hd and pushing it to the ultra preset (rt off) with anti-aliasing.

overall, i absolutely loved the scenery, the magical beasts, and the gameplay logic. the story is a bit mediocre in my opinion, but i really wish we had longer, immersive classes instead of hellish assignments and a lack of a penalty system. that would have made it a full-fledged hogwarts sim. but i suppose they prioritised the aesthetics and narrative over roleplay consequences. still, who am i to complain when they have compiled this much into a single game? 

so yeah, the addition of mods is hilarious and fun. i also really wish a multiplayer mode was included so i could duel with friends. i'm holding out hope that more content, like a new story expansion, will be added later on!

Beautiful game. Loved the gameplay mechanics. I almost completed it 100% but i gave up on doing all merlin trials. If it is on sale and you can run it just buy it. You can improve gear, raid camps, and have beasts in your secret room. As I harry potter fan it was a refreshing taking on the Hogwarts's World. I would have wished for extra stuff to do in the school, but I'm happy with how it is currently set up.

It’s hard for me to say whether I recommend it or not. Ultimately, let’s say I do, though the game has plenty of issues.

Pros:
- This game is an absolute treat for Potterheads. Hogwarts and Hogsmeade look simply fantastic. There are little details and nods to the series everywhere.
- The combat is quite engaging and enjoyable. In my opinion, the PC version could be a bit better optimized for keyboard and mouse.
- Some of the side and main storylines are quite interesting. In my opinion first half of the game is way more charming with attending classes etc.

Cons:
- [i]Revelio, Revelio, Revelio, Revelio[/i]. If players needs to constantly use some kind of special sight your exploration sucks. Period.
- Absolutely awful, disgusting, and unbearable ludonarrative dissonance. Our character is great at everything. Nothing is difficult for them. And of course they are a Chosen One. Plus, as a student who has barely started at Hogwarts, we go around killing Ashwinders, Goblins, and other magical creatures. Add to that the Unforgivable Curses, which you can cast anywhere and they don’t make an impression on anyone.
- Even though the world is filled with NPCs, it feels like a dollhouse. Non-player characters practically don’t react to anything happening around them. Open world feels equally empty. The map is enormous yet the amount of unique content is lost in its vastness.
- The open-world disease. That is, it’s filled with a handful of activities repeated dozens or even hundreds of times. Merlin’s Trials quickly become boring and repetitive. Clearing yet another enemy camp rewards us with nothing more than another trait. Catching magical beasts is just spamming an F so... Adding a mini-game to the Alohomora spell was also a terrible design choice.
- The game’s optimization leaves much to be desired, even on high-end hardware.

I maxed game achievements, but at times I had to clench my teeth as I waded through some of the developers' decisions.

The state of this game is not just pathetic, its an INSULT to anyone who had the misfortune of paying for this.

For context, I first played Hogwarts Legacy on the Series S, and there it ran like a charm and looked the part too, so I ask you, how with my PC being at least twice as powerful and the game having 3 years to 'fix', does it STILL run like this?!
Im sorry, did Port Key games not get enough billions of dollars to justify ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ TRYING???

This joke of software has crashed on me more times than any Bethesda game ive ever played, ive already needed to edit the damn file setting in the Player config folder, simply to get this to run!
Yet AGAIN today when I decided to try and play (emphasis on TRY), HL decided to "prepare shaders" and once again crash 10% of the way! Im so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ over this.

The state of this release on PC is such a blatant insult to the PC gaming community that it genuinely feels personal!
The sheer fact this title made over $2 BILLION yet they clearly didnt give half a damn to even fix the PC port is INSANE!!!

If it isnt clear by now, I dont recommend you waste your money on this game, Port Key clearly didnt either!

For Context: 
Never in all my years playing on PC have I felt such frustration at the sheer lack of effort a studio has put into the state of their release, and thats coming from someone who loved Fallout and The Elder Scrolls!
Perhaps I wouldnt be so mad if the game itself was as bad as this port, but no. HL is a solid 8/10, yet this PC port is so horrid, it brings the whole score down to 3/10 and thats being generous! 

Score:
World Design: 10/10
Audio and immersion: 9/10
Character and story: 8/10
Pacing: 6/10
Performance and Optimization: -9/10

Final rating: 3/10

Id say do better Port Key, but youve clearly had enough years to prove you dont care!

Hogwarts Legacy looks great, but the gameplay gets boring fast. The combat feels clunky, the quests are repetitive, and the open world is filled with tedious filler content

Yeah with that being said, not sure if I will finish it

Its crazy to me to see 91% of positive reviews, theres nothing that this game does well

most basic dialogues i have ever heard

combat system sucks ass i always prefer harder modes in games but here i chose story, just because the game does not respect my time
difficulty levels just controlling how much damage evemy is getting and how much damage you are getting,
so basically on a boss fight its ok to spend 5 mins of doing the same crap over and over again, theres no new move sets, nothing. only the time it takes to kill and even on story difficulty boses are quite lengthy anyways

amount of glazing in this game even chatgpt cant combat

game is not polished at all rough edges can be seen everywhere

game is not too well optimized (only switching to linux helped performance)

there are just 2-3 really interesting quests

at least i am glad i bought for 5 eur and not for 60, 

if you got recommended this game by your white girl friend that doesnt play games much with words 'its an amazing game you should try it' - its a trap game is garbage

if you are a young lady in age of 7-16 and love harry potter you will like it no doubt, otherwise not

Rarely in my life have I had this little fun playing a game. The gameplay was unimaginative, boring, ridiculously easy, incredibly tedious, the map was beautiful but absolutely awful to traverse, the characters were forgettable, the story was uninteresting, the dialogue was horrible. I hated every second of it.
awesome game 10\10 Super Atmospheric, Makes you feel like you're actually in the wizarding world.

easily a 10/10 game for me - as a lifelong harry potter fan, this game was amazing. Incredible detail and the combat was amazingly fun, so many different spell builds and combos to use. Would absolutely recommend.
dialogue so boring i wish i didn't speak english. missions almost as boring. lazy game

3 years late to the party playing this but it is superb.  Incredible amount of content and I am only half way through the main quests. Looks like there is a lot of replayability with unique things for the different houses and different builds. The only think I don't like is it is heavily combat based, everything from potions to plants and beasts, relates to combat.  The combat was fun for a while but it has no variety now, it is the same enemies and the same technique over and over again, which gets boring.  Thankfully the graphics are amazing, even on my old 980Ti.

If you're a Harry Potter fan, this game may be worth your time.

You might not get an literal owl with your Hogwarts acceptance letter, but you can customize your own character to make a belated fifth year entry. Choose your appearance, your house, even your very own Ollivander made wand, attend classes, fly your broom, pet cute magical animals - in terms of a 'adventure at Hogwarts' experience that you always wanted when you were eleven years old, this game delivers.

It also has some good gameplay and quality of life elements:

Figuring out the attack pattern of enemies and countering them perfectly to launch your own barrage of control, force and damage spells feels good. Fast travel points are plentiful, navigating the open world using a broom is swift and using a spell as a [i]interactable element[/i]/[i]interesting location[/i] radar makes exploration fun.

The issues I have with the game are threefold:

[olist]  
    [*]The NPCs are uncanny, both in their facial design and in some of their dialogue (house elves and "good" goblins in particular). The game tries to push the "your cutesy adventure at magic school" angle a bit too much at times, which... well, I would have wished to discover the skip dialogue function earlier, or to be able to listen to it in the background while continuing to explore the world.
    [*] The original story is bland. The game utilizes quite a bit of the magical world the Harry Potter books provide, but many of the bits exclusive to the plot of Hogwarts Legacy are... generic at best, written "like HR is in the room" at worst.
    [*]While graphics, animations and the number of systems in the game are quite impressive and they're generally well polished, they also feel like a matter of "quantity over quality".
[/olist]

Let's take the number of spells in the game as an example. Having more spells is a good thing, right? As befitting a wizard game?
Yes, but also not necessarily? You have a hotbar of four spells at the start of the game, and can unlock up to four four-slot hotbars in total. Sixteen slots for twenty six non-essential spells in total, which leads to quite a bit of hotbar juggling during fights and slot-reassignment for side-activities out of combat. 

Then there is gear. You can find a variety of outfit pieces for your character (hat, cloak, body, headgear, scarf, gloves). Each gives you a bonus to your offensive/defensive stats, so you always want to find better gear and swap out under-leveled pieces. On a positive note, you can swap the appearance of a gear to that of any other piece of the same type you have found/unlocked... the downside being that you have to do so every time you equip something new, as the changed appearance is a modification of each separate gear-piece, not a setting on how you want your character to look like. There is no bulk-sell.

You can also enchant gear with traits, i.e. magical upgrades. Without playing on the hard difficulty, I have found no reason to do so. Dealing more damage with a specific spell  or to a specific enemy type (e.g. spider, goblin) did not seem a worthwhile investment of resources for a gear that I will cycle out an hour later anyway. The game also has no option to display the exact numbers these boosts provide, even though it displays damage numbers in combat.

There is a certain location in the game that can be used to build a base, but compared to proper base-building games (e.g. V Rising), the mechanics are shallow. Arranging and decorating rooms involves multiple spells, furniture does not display an hitbox if it [i]doesn't[/i] fit into the specific corner you want to place it in and the UI seems better suited for a controller than for mouse and keyboard.

Aside from the purely mechanical side of things, the story is lackluster. Dialogue features a "nice" and a "jerk" option, neither of which changes the outcome of conversations aside from the very next sentence of the NPCs (and maybe some additional gold if you threaten to keep the items you fetched for them). Aside from a specific companion's questline and at most a single other sidequest, there are no real decision points in the game. Hogwarts Legacy is an adventure game, not an RPG.

It's a mixed bag, one that gets worse the deeper you reach into it.
Gameplay feels fine,  story is somewhat interesting for what little ive experienced so far, but I cant stand most of the characters I've come across so far, with a small handful of exceptions.
Finde die Gegner Auswahl todeskacke, aber das Gameplay, die Grafik und das erkunden ist super cool. Nicht das Story technisch beste Spiel aber muss man gespielt haben
The gameplay is okay but it's definitely not for me. Plus the optimization is kind of terrible where I have to sit the next 30 minutes figuring out why everything is glitching on my screen.

Even after just 7 hours, Hogwarts Legacy already feels like a love letter to Harry Potter fans. Exploring Hogwarts, learning spells, and flying around the world is genuinely immersive. The castle itself is packed with detail and secrets that make you want to keep exploring. Combat is surprisingly fun and fluid too.

The story starts a bit slow, but once the world opens up, it becomes hard to stop playing. If you enjoy magic, exploration, and RPG progression, this game is definitely worth experiencing.

Hogwarts and Hogsmeade are wonderfully crafted - the sheer amount of detail is easily the best part of the game. The music is incredible, too. You can truly feel the passion and love that went into the architecture, music, and Potter lore.

But overall? It’s a pretty mid game.

The quests and characters are unbelievably dull, and the 'plot twists' are idiotic, even for kindergarteners. Character models also look outdated and rigid. Gameplay is mid at best. Honestly, if you want a decent experience, you need to install 30+ QoL mods just to make the game feel complete.

That being said, I'm glad I played it. In fleeting moments, it still managed to capture that magical fairy-tale feeling from childhood - just like when I first read Harry Potter or watched The Chronicles of Narnia. A great part of that feeling came from Hogsmeade and Hogwarts with that beautiful music, just roaming around and taking everything in.

A nice treat for fans of the series. But unfortunately, it feels like it was designed for younger players. The gameplay is very hand-holding and incredibly simple. That doesn't change the fact that I had a great time.

A magical, captivating experience that will accidentally steal 100 hours of your life.

From the moment you step foot inside the castle, Hogwarts Legacy proves to be an absolute dream come true for anyone who ever wanted an acceptance letter. Aesthetically, the game is a breathtaking masterpiece. The sheer attention to detail—from the shifting staircases and floating candles to the cozy warmth of the common rooms and the sprawling beauty of the Scottish Highlands—makes it one of the most visually pleasing worlds ever crafted. It is so incredibly immersive that you will likely find yourself just wandering the halls, listening to the portraits gossip, and taking in the atmosphere for hours before you even touch the main questline.

What makes the game truly shine is how wonderfully accessible it is. If you aren't typically a fan of complex action-RPGs with overwhelming stat management and frantic dodging, this game strikes the absolute perfect balance. The spellcasting combat is fluid, flashy, and simple enough to grasp quickly, making you feel like a highly capable wizard without needing lightning-fast reflexes or a spreadsheet to optimize your build. It’s incredibly satisfying to string together combos, and the mechanics are straightforward enough that anyone can jump in and have fun immediately.

The only slight bump in the magical road is the puzzle design. While exploring, you will inevitably run into environmental riddles and Merlin Trials that can get surprisingly confusing, to the point where you might need to swallow your wizarding pride and cast a quick "Google search" spell just to figure out what you are supposed to do. But honestly, even that doesn't detract from the joy of the game. The gameplay loop is so inherently rewarding that I found myself hunting down collectibles and accidentally hitting near-100% completion without even realizing it. It just sucks you in that completely.

10/10 — I have cast 'Revelio' so many times I am starting to see hidden objects in my actual living room.

As a potterhead, this game is everything you need to reminisce about old times when we used to wonder when will us go to hogwarts. The graphics, story, settings everything is great. This game delivers fully what was expected of it

The story is meh, the character are almost all meh. Why so linear? Why a story so boring and obvious? The only great part is the castle, where you really feel in the HP world. A part from that and the spells (super satisfying), is pretty boring. Also, the unforgivable spells have no consequences,  so unforgivable for who? Nobody reacts a part for some comments in the background.

>game is called Hogwarts Legacy
>game barely takes place at Hogwarts
You only spend 2 hours in the beginning with classes at Hogwarts, then the rest of the game turns into Ubisoft open world checklist slop. BORING!
As much as I hate J.K.R., this is a good game and I thoroughly enjoyed the immersive world of Hogwarts. It's like a childhood dream come true.

As a longtime fan of the Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone movies and the entire Wizarding World, playing Hogwarts Legacy felt like stepping into a dream I've had since childhood. The game captures the magic and atmosphere of Hogwarts so beautifully that every moment feels nostalgic, especially for fans who grew up watching the films.

One of my favorite parts of the experience was simply walking through the halls of Hogwarts Castle. The attention to detail is incredible — from the moving staircases to the cozy common rooms and magical classrooms, everything feels alive and faithful to the world I fell in love with years ago. Exploring the castle never got old because there was always something new to discover, whether it was hidden secrets, magical paintings, or familiar locations from the movies.

What made the experience even more special was getting to play as another student attending Hogwarts. Instead of watching Harry’s journey, I was able to create my own story and truly feel like part of the school. Attending classes, learning spells, flying around the world on a broom, and building my own wizard identity made the experience immersive and personal.

The combat, exploration, and world design kept me engaged for hours, but the emotional connection to the Potter universe is what really made the game unforgettable for me. It brought back the same sense of wonder I felt watching the films for the first time.

I genuinely hope the developers continue supporting the game with more DLCs or future expansions because the Wizarding World they created deserves to grow even bigger. There’s still so much potential for new stories, locations, and adventures, and I would gladly return to Hogwarts again and again.
I loved the mods, It's a lot of work to play and buggy but it;ll tide us over

[h1]A shallow experience in a beautifully built world[/h1]

6.5/10

The game might be more fun if it's your first RPG, but after playing much more interesting titles I find it quite disappointing. The world design looks great, the character customization is pretty good and combat can be fun; these are the only positive traits I can think of about the game. The game feels kinda empty and soulless, with the same bunch of items you can find in the wild throughout the whole map. The "puzzles" are too easy, the quests are quite boring and feel meaningless. The writing and storytelling is quite unimaginative; this game like many other pieces of media does not understand the concept of "show don't tell". The game takes itself too seriously and the characters are plain, without much of a personality, which shows in the boring dialogues. Most characters have the personality of a spoon and constantly and unnecessarily praise the MC for how special and talented and amazing they are, which is pretty annoying. The story is bland and very few of your choice and actions actually seem to matter (despite it being an RPG). As many have said, this game is as wide as an ocean and as deep as a puddle. Definitely don't buy it at full price.

this game is just so boring. on my first play through i just got bored and stopped playing for like a year then downloaded it again and did a new game. news flash ITS SO BORING!!! i just cant. it was fun at first exploring Hogwarts but i just cant force myself to keep playing. 

also for steam deck users, game runs fine. there's lag spikes here and there. 

so if you don't care about story all that much and just wanna explore Hogwarts. this is the game for you.

I can't really recommend it. Characters are very empty, especially the main character. The dialogue and voice acting is awful, everything is overly positive.

Combat is good, and it's fun to explore, but that's not enough to hold interest.

Your choices don't matter, and it doesn't even feel like you're a student at hogwarts.

I've tried to get through this game but with nothing that grips me, I find it very difficult to be interested.

It feels like the game is riddled with side quests and things to collect in order to keep the player busy instead of filling it with good storytelling (the best part of HP).

the use of simple terms is annoying and so basic and the "challenges" are just dungeons which is something i dont like, and ofc the combat system and magic is something u would find in every rpg game and it shows how generic it is 7/10 idk who likes this gay AAA ♥♥♥♥
Open world game where you're a student at Hogwarts. It's not perfect, but it's awesome and cozy. Also mods now. :)
I am loving it so far. The world is beautiful and feels alive. There is a lot to explore and the quests are interesting. 10/10

If you can ignore the lack of UK people in early 1890s UK and the lack of good writing in this game based on an acclaimed book series it's a fun wizard game made even better by turning off the dialog and subtitles and adding a dozen mods to make up for the developers. Well worth the money, if you waited for a sale.
A no-brainer if you like the harry potter/"wizarding world."  Also its an independently great open world with a solid story and good game play.

This game requires kernel level access to your PC despite being SINGLE PLAYER, and caused steam to malfunction and stutter even after it had been uninstalled for some time. I don't have an old PC and I've never seen it respond to software this way. The anticheat on this game is Denuvo, which is known for its detrimental effect on performance and terrible security (I wasn't aware of this at the time of download, as it isn't advertised anywhere, but became concerned while reading the eula and terms, so I'll put this out there for anyone else considering it to see.)

Download at your own risk.

I’m not super familiar with the Harry Potter universe, but i watched all the movies for the first time recently and i love open-world games, so i had to try this. It was also on sale for $10 bucks. For starters, the character creation is okay, but the voice customization sucks. Anything other than the standard pitch is the same as the standard voice, only overlaid with a low-quality pitch of that same voice. The combat is fun though, talents feel useful, and swapping between different spells & spell sets is easy. The movement is also something i can’t complain about. Your character will actually take bigger steps to traverse uneven terrain while maintaining speed, which feels & looks good. Flying is also very simple, which is a relief. Overall movement & fighting definitely feel like the more polished aspects of the game as opposed to, well, everything else. 
The world itself is very pretty, but there’s a lot of stuttering and fps drops, even when i turn my graphics down. The environment is detailed but, outside of cutscenes, npc’s look more plastic than human & don’t move their mouth when speaking half the time. Most dialogue “choices” aren’t really choices, but rather 2 options, 1 of which won’t actually progress the task you’re on, forcing you to just choose the option the game wants. The choices you do have are simply making someone give you more money for a task, with absolutely zero downside or impact on the story. Speaking of the story, it’s okay. The main quests & cutscenes are kinda interesting, but everything else is pretty bland. There’s a reason i was using the word “tasks” earlier. Also, the voice acting isn't bad but 99% of the students are legitimately insufferable, save Natsai, Sebastian, & Poppy. It’s so blatant, i can only imagine it was intentional and the devs were playing into the whole “snotty brats because fancy school” thing, just with every single student you meet. The professors, on the other hand, are tolerable. The puzzles & minigames throughout Hogwarts Legacy were either thought up in 10 minutes or designed for small children. Fortunately, the classes are pleasant & quick. I was gonna try for 100% once i beat the main story, but this game is too boring. 
This last part might be an unpopular opinion but it goes for all open-world games: the world feels big until i unlock all the fast travel points and it just isn’t anymore. Not only does teleporting to any location after being there once make the “big” world feel small & fake, but it also makes the brooms & mounts feel totally redundant. Sure, a few fast travel points at major locations, but over 80? Not to mention the other open-world games that have hundreds. Why walk, or even fly, when i can just open the map? Anyways, i was genuinely surprised to see the “very positive” on it’s steam page, especially after 100,000+ reviews. However, after reading through a lot of them, it’s pretty clear how something this mediocre gets a thumbs up from so many people; the great majority haven’t played a half-decent open world game and/or were just happy to have a Harry Potter game. The only way i would EVER recommend this game is if it’s HEAVILY discontinued and you LOVE Harry Potter.
Mediocre gameplay and incredibly poorly optimized for what it is.

I have never watched the movies or read the books and I think harry looks stupid so I never plan to, but this game caught my eye because it has; 1. character creation and customization.
2. Based in the past so you don’t run into harry, his friends, or his story
3.  Open world game for exploration, battle(that is NOT turn based) and silliness 
4. magic and fun gameplay 
5. you can create your own room?!
6. controller accessible 
All in all, great game, beautiful scenery and graphics. Camera angles during fighting can be annoying but passable. The story is pretty good and keeps me interested. Too many characters introduce themselves with ‘by the way’ which is just annoying. You cant skip dialogues which is lame but fair enough. I would never have got this for full price just because of the controversy of whats her face, but more than just her is involved in the creation of this, and I think that’s important to remember.
Wanted to play this game after a long time not playing it and then the game just lost all my save files. Steam also don't have the save files. I wont be playing the game all over again
90% satisfied with this game, because im a potterhead, always. But maybe make some option for clothing and the face detailing
Beside Merlin trials, it's fun. Go grab the wand and explore, get out of here and buy it already you will have no regrets!
10/10! Stunning visuals, beautiful writing, and even better fight scenes!

Hogwarts Legacy is a great open-world game that really captures the atmosphere and feeling of the Wizarding World.

Combat was fun throughout the game. There are lots of different spells, mechanics, combos, and traits that allow you to enhance your playstyle. Enemy shields and combat feats also force you to use different spell types, which adds variety and keeps combat from becoming too repetitive.

The world feels very alive. Hogwarts, Hogsmeade, and the rest of the open world are filled with places to explore, including rooms, houses, villages, structures, dens, challenges, loot, collectibles, characters, and enemies. There is always something to find, and the environments are very detailed.

The story was engaging, even for someone like me who does not usually focus that much on story. Dialogue choices also felt somewhat meaningful, which helped make the experience more interesting.

Graphically, the game looks amazing. The music, voice acting, and overall audio design are also great and really help build the atmosphere.

The pacing was good during the main story, but after finishing it, the remaining side content became a bit too much. Challenges and collectibles started to feel repetitive, and some things were not very easy to find or keep track of.

I also ran into some technical issues. Going through doors was not always smooth, cloth physics acted up sometimes, and the final Crossed Wands round was a bit buggy and frustrating. Even on a high-end PC, I had some performance issues and had to lower my settings. I also noticed occasional Z-fighting, and lighting sometimes broke when entering houses during daytime, making interiors extremely bright.

Overall, Hogwarts Legacy is definitely worth playing. It is immersive, beautiful, and fun, with a lot of content. I would recommend it, but because of the high price, it might be worth waiting for a sale.

What is there to say about a game that lets you live out your fantasy of being a student at Hogwarts?

The graphics are beautiful, the progression is varied and interesting, the story is captivating and the combat is as challenging as you want it to be, and the music is the perfect companion for your trails and tribulations.

Thoroughly enjoy it and will actually one day finish it, rather than starting a whole new playthrough around the middle of the story, something I am guilty of in pretty much every game I play lol
if you buy it on discount (10€) its 100% worht it, but for full price (60ish€) i dont think its quite enough. Still very fun to play and looks gorgeous!!
very fun game got this game ages ago and still come back to play again

Once you get past the horrible stutterinig in some (many) areas, the game is great.

Story is 7/10 and it has some bad performance issues but for some reason, in this game, I can look past those problems. Once you do, it's a soolid 8.5/10 or even 9/10. And as a bonus, it's breathtakengly beautiful at times.

It's a fantastic game! Fun mechanics, stunning graphics, great customization, and tons of things to do. The combat system is excellent, with a lot of variety.

Soaring through the skies on your broom is absolutely breathtaking...

There’s still room for improvement, of course, like a morality meter linked to the dark arts would’ve been amazing, and I really missed that, but it’s still a great game overall.

As the game opens, you see all the potential. Then it winds down into somewhat tedious missions to learn various skills, paths, etc. It's not until about the 8-10th hour that the game opens up. Still well worth playing.
If your a big fan of the Harry Potter and anything to do with Magic, Wizards/witches and fantasy this is the game for you.

I love the game but it overheats my computer a lot. I've tried everything I can but ultimately, I don't think it should be getting up to 90-100 celsius when running the game. I've never had this issue with any other game of similar or higher quality.

Take this with a pinch of salt though as my computer specs are hardly cutting edge anymore, but still quite disappointing.

I still plan to finish it though..

I'm not much for action, quick-time events, etc., but this game really brings the Wizarding World to life in a way Lego Harry Potter can't come close to. I love the cozy vibe of it all, even if there is a little life-or-death involved!

Such a nice game! Its amazing how much love they put into this, the game looks and plays great! The  main story is kinda meh, but the rest is very enjoyable :) Soo many side quests as well as other things that you can discover and cultivate (like plants and animals!). Would recommend :)
Pretty good game, although could be better in some areas.
I think the gameplay and the lore are overall really well done.
Very well thought game and very well designed. It feels that it was made with passion.

I liked the storyline and the goblins and the way they implemented magic into the game. It was pretty fun, but i did get some low frame rates when I booted up the game for the first 20 minutes. There's so many fun things to do in Hogwarts Legacy, but after playing it for the first time for 3 hours, I can say it was a good experience.
Good game, ok-ish performance, Denuvo sucks, but it has a lot of activities and it's making you feel like the next prodigee Wizard

Hella addicting and fun. Trying to 100% this entire game could take super long, so there's lots to do. Interesting storyline to follow along with, and entertaining NPCs. Overall good game with amazing graphic design.

Not a big Harry Potter fan, yet this game was still really fun.

Graphics: 10/10
Story: 7/10 (A bit drawn-out and/or repetitive in some story lines)
Difficulty: 6.5/10 (Really repetitive once you have unlocked all the good abilities. Then you just spam those till your foe dies)
Fun Factor: 10/10 (Main reason I would recommend it)
Content Amount: Easily enough for 80-90 hours. If you go slower, 100 hours is very possible.

i love this game so far.  I am not a big potter movie fan.  I seen em all, dont hate em,  either.  but this game  is gorgeous, and a lot of fun.  got it for 9 bucks on steam.  85% off  best 9 bucks i ever spent on steam.

I only continued to play this bc as an HP geek I am willing to eat slop to an extent. This game is woke slop that is expectedly lazy, buggy and diversity-riddled in the usual ways. The only nice thing I can say is WB still know how to meticulously build a physical world based on lore, barring minor mistakes. The story is a bit ridiculous and inconsistent with regards to Dark Magic especially, implying even dabbling can be damning, particularly for a friend in a side quest that sees you return to mass murdering people who may or may not be relatives of your classmates in the woods for "poaching". Technically it is functional but poorly optimized and buggy. Overall would have been decent had the story writers not been leftists incapable of relating to normal people, abstaining from proselytizing their ideology once again and every other character being a POC in 1890's Scotland. 4/10

Beautiful environments, fun combat and a large map to explore.

Sometimes the dialogue feels a little stiff and the quests can be repetitive, however overall it's an enjoyable experience. At times, you do get that sense of "magic" the Harry Potter books and films give you. Storyline is engaging at times, but also becomes slightly lacklustre.

Recommend purchasing when it's on sale!
epic  example of modern gamming that is totally immersive, very polished and with a completed feel to it. the story line is also right on its like your there.
Slow start, however very detailed game. Exploring the castle and world shows how much work was put into making this beautiful environment.
I love every aspect of this game. If you love Harry Potter movies and/or magic and sorcery then this game is perfect for you.
games good, but if ur wanting to get 100% achievements it can be extremely tedious with long boring achievements
Fun game, love to revisit and play in this world. Hope for a sequel to go out side Hogwarts and grounds.

Because this isn't about harry potter, it is about you getting into hogwarts and learning spells, fighting enemies and learning ancient spells in your 5th year in hogwars, so that is why i would recommend you this game if you are a harry potter fan.

got this game on sale, REALLY worth the money if u love harry potter

best part is you get to pet cats, 10/10

Hogwarts Legacy deliverers on the long-held dream of exploring and living in the Harry Potter universe with beautiful visuals and engaging gameplay. If you're into magic ,exploration, epic duels and a great story then you wont be disappointed.

I absolutely loved this game. It is one of my favorite games I have ever played along w/ Witcher 3. I'm so excited for the next one to come out. I love these types of games. I played on the hardest mode & I really enjoyed it. It's been months since I beat it & I still dream about playing it again.

Doesn't run too well on steam deck, I experienced consistent frame drops a input lag just running around the school. I sat in the settings menu trying all sorts of different combo of graphic settings, even on the lowest everything the games frame rate is really rough.
Love the game as a whole, immersive game play and open world is amazing. The challenges are good, really enjoying so far!

For the records I'm playing offline (no need to explain why) and Steam stops tracking your playtime when you do that, I'm close to 30hs now which isn't much honestly.

"Haters" convinced me this was a bad game, I believed them sadly.

HL is a 10/10 "Harry Potter" game and more than decent traditional game (call it RPG, immersive SIM, whatever you want, don't care I just like it as much as Elden Ring, Final Fantasy Rebirth, Sonic Frontiers, Silksong, etc).

[h3]What the game does well is being mid at everything it tries to do making the overall experience balanced and fun, which most developers aren't doing nowadays instead focusing in a single thing, FROM gameplay, Sony visuals, Square music, Bethesda jank, etc... 
What it does wrong is not fulfilling every single fantasy in the head of most devoted fans of the franchise (which game does that anyway?), and not having all the DEI some people would want it to have (even if it has more than enough), but because this last topic is super polarizing I'll just apologies and refrain for further mention.
I play with the Japanese dub, mind you, the acting performance and delivery is top-notch even with the same 3 goofy cycling animations during dialogues (yes like Skyrim)... Is fun, is "immersive", is pick and play, has sh1t-ton of content and flying feels better than walking[/h3]
Game that I love to replay over and over. Love the quests and experiencing Hogwarts. If you never got your Hogwarts letter, this is the game to play

I Just got the game on the promo sale, which was an awesome price. I completely am completely immersed. I love all the little easter eggs from the books and movies. So far the game play is great and the story is wonderful.
Pretty good, but got very repetitive. It took a bit of modding to get performance sorted out, but once that was fixed, the game ran great for the entire 55 hours I played it.

I only recently started and have enjoyed myself immensely so far.....that said,
A word of newbie "wizarding wisdom", do not ignore the floo powder buttons you will pass as you explore Hogwarts in the beginning. I found myself having to restart the game when, after exploring Hogsmeade the fast travel mode is needed to get back to the Common Room.
Not a huge deal. Happy to enjoy the first 4 or 5 hours again. Just an observation.

The game itself is really great and the best one with the mods, but I always having error to sync with cloud time to time.
Cutscene after cutscene with almost no gameplay in between. I'm very bored and need to force myself to play it. I'm not seeing myself to finishing it and I'm too deep in for refund.

This game is pretty peak so far after playing it for 15ish hours. It feels like finally something different that's also good. Story is interesting, atmosphere is awesome, combat is good. There's a lot of system mechanics. World is vast. 

Works great with uevr mod too if your a vr player. I choose to just play it with a Xbox controller in VR and it's great.

If you can get it at the sale price of $9 it's an insta buy. I'd probably say it's even worth full price.
